Savannah's Auto Detailing Perfectionists

Horizon Auto Detailing delivers museum-quality results for Savannah's discerning car owners. Our climate-specific treatments combat coastal corrosion while preserving your vehicle's integrity.

What We Do

Our Services

Coastal Ceramic Coating

Historic District Interior Restoration

SEE ALL SERVICES →

Who We Are

Precision Born From Coastal Necessity

Founded in 2012 after witnessing Savannah's salt air ravage classic cars, Horizon Auto Detailing combines scientific paint analysis with artisan techniques. Our team trains quarterly with marine-grade coating specialists to address the unique challenges of Lowcountry vehicle care.

We approach each vehicle like conservators restoring a historic Savannah home - identifying vulnerabilities, using archival-quality products, and documenting every millimeter. The humidity that warps downtown shutters demands equally meticulous protection for your car's interior.

As members of the Savannah Historic Vehicle Society, we sponsor youth programs teaching automotive preservation at the Ships of the Sea Museum. Every ceramic coating includes a donation to hurricane recovery efforts at the Georgia Sea Turtle Center.

12+ Years
800+ Projects
97% Satisfaction
GET IN TOUCH →

Our Difference

Why Choose Horizon Auto Detailing

Salt Air Specialists

Our proprietary 7-step corrosion barrier system outperforms factory protections against Savannah's coastal elements.

Microclimate Mapping

Neighborhood-specific treatments account for downtown humidity variations versus beachfront salt exposure.

Historic District Approved

The only detailing service permitted to work on vehicles displayed during Savannah Tour of Homes events.

Hurricane Prep Certified

Emergency storm protection packages include verified water intrusion prevention for garage-kept vehicles.

Schedule Your Coastal Protection Assessment

Limited same-week appointments available for Forsyth Park-area residents through June.

Get a Free Estimate Call 555-aut-r90

Client Feedback

What Our Clients Say

“

After my Mercedes developed that pinkish haze common to Ardsley Park cars, Horizon's coating not only restored the original silver but survived two hurricane seasons without a single spot. They even noticed corrosion starting on my trunk latch that my mechanic missed.

Eleanor R. Coastal Ceramic Coating
“

When Hurricane Idalia threatened, Horizon stayed open 36 hours straight prepping vehicles. Their temporary glass film saved my windshield from a flying palmetto branch on I-16. Post-storm, they documented all damage for State Farm while removing the protective coatings.

Marcus T. Hurricane Evacuation Prep
“

The team perfectly matched the original seersucker headliner in my 1963 Thunderbird convertible, using fabric samples from when the car was sold new at Victor Chevrolet on Broughton Street. Their humidity-controlled storage recommendation prevented further mold damage in my carriage house garage.

Lillian P. Historic District Interior Restoration
“

As a sales manager at Genesis of Savannah, I insist every vehicle gets Horizon's prep. Their attention to transport film removal and undercarriage cleaning has eliminated nearly all our PDI comebacks. Clients routinely comment how our vehicles look better than Miami showrooms.

Theodore W. Luxury Dealership Pre-Delivery Detailing
“

My white Model Y was turning pink from marsh gases near the Moon River bridge. Horizon's correction process not only restored the color but their quarterly touch-ups have kept it pristine through two pollen seasons. Worth every penny for Savannah residents.

Clara B. Savannah-Specific Paint Correction
“

The ceramic coating on my fishing truck has withstood weekly trips to Tybee boat ramps for 18 months without fading. Horizon's team even applied extra protection to my trailer lights after noticing how often they're submerged in saltwater.

Julian H. Coastal Ceramic Coating

Our Portfolio

Our Recent Work

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

How does this differ from regular ceramic coatings?
Standard ceramics fail within 18 months against Savannah's salt air. Our formula incorporates marine epoxy resins used on River Street barges, with UV stabilizers tested at the Skidaway Institute of Oceanography.
Will this prevent hurricane flood damage?
While not waterproof, our coating creates a hydrophobic barrier that significantly slows saltwater penetration during temporary immersion - we've documented 72-hour protection during 2017's Irma flooding.
